Title: Silicon-induced alterations in the expression of aquaporins and antioxidant system activity in well-watered and drought-stressed oilseed rape

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The study did not detect a clear, linear correlation between external silicon presence and BnPIP1 accumulation, indicating that additional factors (plant age, formulation with Fe and other components) modulate its response to silicon.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. BnPIP1 protein is present in leaves of well-watered oilseed rape plants and is detectable as a specific band in immunoblots.
2. In control and silicon-complex-treated plants, BnPIP1 accumulation is significantly higher in the oldest plants compared with younger ones, with the maximum after three silicon-complex applications.
3. Plants watered only with silicon do not show this age-dependent increase in BnPIP1, and overall no simple correlation between BnPIP1 accumulation and silicon presence was established.
4. These patterns indicate that BnPIP1 is part of the silicon-responsive aquaporin network regulating leaf water management, particularly under optimal watering.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Leaf plasma-membrane aquaporin whose accumulation is enhanced by repeated silicon-complex supplementation in older, well-watered plants, indicating a role in silicon-modulated regulation of leaf water transport under non-stress conditions, although no simple correlation with silicon alone was observed.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: BnPIP1 protein accumulation is modulated by plant age and the presence of silicon complex, suggesting post-transcriptional or stability-based regulation rather than a simple dependence on external silicon concentration.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Silicon-responsive regulation of aquaporin-mediated leaf water transport under non-stress conditions.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aquaporin, plasma membrane intrinsic protein (PIP1) water channel family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: BnPIP1 protein is detectable in leaves under well-watered conditions; its accumulation increases with plant age in control and silicon-complex-treated plants, with the highest level after three applications of the silicon complex, whereas plants watered only with silicon do not show this age-dependent increase.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Silicon supplementation as orthosilicic acid or Optysil silicon+Fe complex under well-watered conditions, with repeated applications at weekly intervals.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Regulation of leaf aquaporin accumulation and water management by silicon supplementation in oilseed rape under well-watered and drought conditions.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress – drought stress; water balance and aquaporin-mediated water transport; oxidative stress-related responses under silicon nutrition.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Leaves of oilseed rape (Brassica napus var. napus L.) cv. Markus plants grown in pots under well-watered conditions and supplied three times with water (control), orthosilicic acid (silicon), or Optysil silicon+Fe complex.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Protein extraction from leaves, SDS-PAGE, immunoblotting with anti-PIP1 antibody, NBT/BCIP staining, densitometric analysis of band intensity; real-time quantitative PCR for aquaporin transcripts using Actin as reference.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein-level detection and semi-quantitative analysis by immunoblotting with PIP1-specific antibody and densitometry in silicon-treated versus control plants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known Brassica napus aquaporin gene; primers designed from published sequences and expression analyzed by immunoblotting and real-time PCR.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: BnPIP1 responsiveness to silicon+Fe supplementation under well-watered conditions indicates that agronomic use of silicon complexes could be used to fine-tune leaf aquaporin abundance and water transport capacity in oilseed rape, potentially improving hydration and vigor in high-input systems, though its direct contribution to drought mitigation is less clear than for other aquaporins.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The study highlights a marked decoupling between BnPIP2-1-7 transcript and protein levels under drought with silicon complex, consistent with stress- and silicon-dependent post-translational regulation of aquaporins; both monomeric and dimeric forms contribute to the total protein signal.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. BnPIP2-1-7 protein (monomers and dimers) is present in leaves of oilseed rape and its accumulation increases with plant age under well-watered conditions in control and silicon-complex-treated plants, with the highest level after three applications of silicon complex.
2. In silicon-only plants under well-watered conditions, the age-dependent pattern is reversed, with highest BnPIP2-1-7 accumulation in the youngest plants.
3. Under drought, silicon supplementation (both sources) significantly reduces BnPIP2-1-7 protein accumulation compared to control plants, while silicon complex increases BnPIP2-1-7 transcript levels.
4. These contrasting protein and transcript responses support a role for BnPIP2-1-7 as a key silicon-responsive aquaporin regulating leaf water transport, with downregulation of protein abundance under drought interpreted as a mechanism to minimize water loss and maintain turgor.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Leaf PIP2 aquaporin considered a highly efficient water transporter whose protein accumulation is strongly modulated by silicon and drought: silicon complex enhances BnPIP2-1-7 in well-watered, older plants, while under drought silicon reduces its protein level relative to control, consistent with silicon-mediated restriction of plasma-membrane water permeability to maintain leaf turgor during water deficit.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Under drought, silicon treatments decrease BnPIP2-1-7 protein abundance while silicon complex simultaneously elevates its transcript level, indicating silicon-dependent post-transcriptional and/or post-translational downregulation of this aquaporin to limit plasma-membrane water permeability during water deficit.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Silicon-mediated drought response pathway influencing aquaporin-dependent leaf water transport and water conservation.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aquaporin, plasma membrane intrinsic protein (PIP2) water channel family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under well-watered conditions, BnPIP2-1-7 protein (monomers and dimers) accumulates to higher levels in leaves of older plants, with the highest accumulation after three applications of silicon complex; in silicon-only plants, the highest accumulation occurs in the youngest plants. Under drought, both monomeric and dimeric BnPIP2-1-7 are present, but silicon supplementation significantly reduces total protein accumulation relative to control plants, while silicon complex concurrently increases BnPIP2-1-7 transcript abundance.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought versus well-watered soil moisture (30% vs 65–70% field water capacity) combined with silicon supplementation as orthosilicic acid or Optysil silicon+Fe complex.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of PIP2 aquaporins in silicon-mediated regulation of leaf water transport and drought/oxidative stress responses in oilseed rape.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress – drought stress; water balance and hydraulic conductivity; oxidative stress tolerance under silicon supplementation.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Leaves of 54-day-old oilseed rape (Brassica napus var. napus L.) cv. Markus plants grown in pots under well-watered (65–70% field water capacity) or drought (30% field water capacity) conditions, supplemented with water (control), orthosilicic acid (silicon), or Optysil silicon+Fe complex.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Leaf protein extraction and SDS-PAGE, immunoblotting with anti-PIP2-1-7 antibody, NBT/BCIP detection and densitometric analysis of monomer and dimer bands; RNA isolation, cDNA synthesis, and real-time quantitative PCR to measure BnPIP2-1-7 transcript levels relative to Actin.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional involvement inferred from protein and transcript responses to silicon and drought treatments demonstrated by immunoblotting of BnPIP2-1-7 monomers and dimers and qRT-PCR of BnPIP2-1-7 transcripts in leaves under well-watered and drought conditions.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known Brassica napus aquaporin gene BnPIP2-1-7; primers designed from GenBank cDNA sequence and expression quantified by real-time PCR, protein detected with PIP2-1-7-specific antibody.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because BnPIP2-1-7 is a highly efficient water channel whose protein level is downregulated by silicon under drought, management or breeding strategies that favor silicon responsiveness of this gene could help reduce transpirational water loss and maintain leaf turgor in oilseed rape during water deficit, while silicon complex-induced upregulation under non-stress conditions may support efficient water uptake and growth.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: BnTIP1;1 is highlighted as a tonoplast aquaporin marker for temporary soil water shortage; silicon complex increases its accumulation under both well-watered and drought conditions, and these changes occur alongside enhanced SOD activity and non-enzymatic antioxidant capacity in silicon-treated plants, linking BnTIP1;1 to coordinated water and redox management.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. BnTIP1;1 tonoplast aquaporin is present as monomeric and dimeric forms in leaves under both well-watered and drought conditions.
2. Under well-watered conditions, silicon complex significantly increases BnTIP1;1 protein accumulation compared with control and silicon-only treatments, and transcript levels show a similar trend.
3. Under drought, silicon supplementation from both sources significantly elevates BnTIP1;1 protein accumulation relative to control plants, whereas BnTIP1;1 transcripts are highest in control and reduced by silicon, especially silicon complex.
4. These results support BnTIP1;1 as a key silicon-responsive aquaporin involved in maintaining water balance and contributing to the mitigation of drought-induced stress in oilseed rape, particularly in response to silicon complex supplementation.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tonoplast aquaporin whose accumulation is enhanced by silicon complex in well-watered leaves and by silicon from both sources under drought; under drought, higher BnTIP1;1 protein abundance in silicon-treated plants despite reduced transcripts suggests a silicon-mediated stabilization or increased translation of this TIP, contributing to maintenance of vacuolar water status and improved drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Under drought, silicon treatments increase BnTIP1;1 protein accumulation while reducing transcript levels relative to control, suggesting silicon-dependent post-transcriptional regulation (enhanced protein stability or translation) that maintains tonoplast aquaporin abundance during water deficit.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Silicon-mediated drought response pathway affecting tonoplast aquaporin-dependent water balance and associated antioxidant defense.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aquaporin, tonoplast intrinsic protein (TIP1) water channel family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: BnTIP1;1 monomers and dimers are clearly detectable in leaves under both well-watered and drought conditions. Under well-watered conditions, BnTIP1;1 protein accumulation is significantly higher in silicon-complex-treated plants than in control or silicon-only plants. Under drought, silicon supplementation from either source markedly increases BnTIP1;1 protein accumulation compared to control. Transcript levels under well-watered conditions follow the protein pattern (highest in silicon-complex plants), whereas under drought the highest BnTIP1;1 transcript level is in control plants and is reduced by silicon, particularly by silicon complex.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought versus well-watered conditions combined with silicon supplementation as orthosilicic acid or Optysil silicon+Fe complex.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of tonoplast aquaporin BnTIP1;1 in silicon-mediated water management and drought/oxidative stress responses in oilseed rape.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress – drought stress; water balance and vacuolar/osmotic adjustment; oxidative stress tolerance under silicon supplementation.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Leaves of 54-day-old oilseed rape (Brassica napus var. napus L.) cv. Markus plants grown in pots under well-watered (65–70% field water capacity) or drought (30% field water capacity) conditions, with or without silicon supplementation (orthosilicic acid or Optysil silicon+Fe complex).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Total protein extraction and SDS-PAGE, immunoblotting with anti-TIP1 antibody, NBT/BCIP detection and densitometric analysis of monomer and dimer bands; RNA isolation, cDNA synthesis, and real-time quantitative PCR to measure BnTIP1;1 transcripts relative to Actin.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein and transcript responses to silicon and drought demonstrated by immunoblotting of TIP1 monomers and dimers and qRT-PCR of BnTIP1;1 transcripts in leaves under well-watered and drought conditions.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Known Brassica napus tonoplast aquaporin gene BnTIP1;1; primers designed from GenBank cDNA sequence and expression quantified by real-time PCR, protein detected with TIP1-specific antibody.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because BnTIP1;1 protein is consistently enhanced by silicon supplementation, especially silicon complex, under drought and is proposed to support water balance and oxidative stress mitigation, it represents a promising molecular target or marker for breeding oilseed rape with improved silicon-responsive drought tolerance; agronomic silicon fertilization that upregulates or stabilizes BnTIP1;1 could improve survival and performance under water deficit.
